commit.message:
docs: Add VMWare Workstation 25H2 docs, chore: restructure content (#41)

## Summary

Global restructuring and additions of content. Solved a few bugs, added
a new section about running VMWare Workstation 25H2 on Linux. Corrected
some typo's, moved content. etc.

## Type of change

<!-- Check all that apply -->

- [x] `fix` — bug fix (broken link, incorrect command, layout issue)
- [x] `content` — update or improve existing content
- [x] `docs` — changes to CONTRIBUTING, README, or meta documentation
- [x] `chore` — maintenance (dependencies, config, CI/CD)

> [PR title and commit types must follow these standards — view the
contributing
guide](https://github.com/Stensel8/Zephyrus-Linux/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#commit-messages)

## Checklist

- [x] PR title follows the commit convention (e.g. `fix: correct nmcli
command in eduroam guide`)
- [x] Both EN and NL versions updated (if applicable)
- [x] Media is in AVIF format (not PNG/JPG)
- [x] No broken image references (`/images/*.avif` all exist in
`static/images/`)
- [x] Tested locally with `hugo server`

---------

commit.message:
fix: swap mssql/server for azure-sql-edge on ARM64 hosts

The Azure Service Bus emulator depends on SQL Server, which has no ARM64
image. On Apple Silicon (and other ARM64 hosts), the mssql/server:2022-latest
container fails under QEMU with jemalloc address mapping errors.

Use Aspire's BeforeStartEvent to intercept the container model and swap the
image to azure-sql-edge:latest, which has native ARM64 support. The Service
Bus emulator works with either SQL engine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add .http files for eShop REST API

Structured by bounded context with auth placeholders:
- catalog.http  — 5 endpoints (brands, types, items, item by id, update price)
- basket.http   — 4 endpoints (get, update, checkout, delete)
- ordering.http — 5 endpoints (list, get by id, create, cancel, ship)
- _variables.http — shared IDs and health check
- http-client.env.json — dev/aspire environment config

Authorization headers are commented out, ready to uncomment when auth is added.

Adopt Deepstaging.Aspire for generated Service Bus topology

Replace 17 lines of manual AddServiceBusTopic/AddServiceBusSubscription calls
with a single ServiceBusTopology.Configure(serviceBus) call that is auto-generated
from [IntegrationEvents] and [IntegrationEventHandler] metadata.

- Add Deepstaging.Aspire package reference to eShop.Aspire
- Add bounded context projects as compile refs (IsAspireProjectResource=false)
  so the Aspire generator can discover event metadata
- EmitCompilerGeneratedFiles for IDE visibility of generated topology
- Fix subscription name: ordering-own-event-handlers (matches handler class name)

Bug fixes found while making tests pass:

  jsapi_ticket→js_api_ticket to match GORM snake_case column generation
- Fix studyRecordService.Update: derive CourseID from UnitID via repo
  lookup instead of leaving it 0 (caused FK constraint violation)

* refactor: replace config files with env vars; remove Dockerfile.api-test and tests/api/config.yaml

- Rename configs/config.yaml -> configs/config.yaml.sample (template only)
- Dockerfile: remove COPY of configs/ directory from image

- docker-compose.api-test.yml: use main Dockerfile, supply all APP_* env
  vars, override healthcheck to keep fast poll timing
- docker-compose.yml: drop ./configs volume mount, add missing env vars
- internal/app/config.go: add defaults for all keys, make config file
  optional (skip ReadInConfig when configPath is empty)
- cmd/server/main.go: remove hardcoded fallback to configs/config.yaml

Co-authored-by: luoxiaojun1992 <6905700+luoxiaojun1992@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add SetEnvKeyReplacer so nested APP_* env vars are read correctly

Viper's AutomaticEnv() upper-cases keys as-is, producing APP_DATABASE.HOST
(with a literal dot) instead of APP_DATABASE_HOST. Without a replacer, the
env var lookup always fails and the default value (localhost) is used.

Fix: v.SetEnvKeyReplacer(strings.NewReplacer(".", "_"))

Also adds config_test.go with tests for env-var-only and default-value loading.

Co-authored-by: luoxiaojun1992 <6905700+luoxiaojun1992@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: scan report panel clickability + scan retry on timeout

Two fixes:

1. Scan Report Panel was not opening on click:
   - Moved panel div inside the last-scan span (correct DOM nesting)
   - Added event.stopPropagation() to prevent parent click interference
   - Fixed close-on-outside-click to check parent container

2. Scan Phase 1 timeout now retries with reduced context:
   - On first timeout (180s), creates fresh SDK session
   - Reduces task context from 80 to 20 tasks
   - Reduces scan range from N to min(N, 2) days
   - Logs both attempts with timing for diagnostics
   - If both fail, returns 502 with descriptive error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Normalize invalid AEGIS values

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: order chunked images by pending job count descending

get_pending_image_ids() was ordering by image_id ASC, causing chunk 1
to contain nearly-complete images (only perception pending). Since
perception is deferred to after all chunks, the scheduler had nothing
to do and moved to chunk 2 immediately.

Now orders by COUNT(pending jobs) DESC so images needing the most work
go first. Chunk 1 now gets fresh images with all 7 modules pending,
ensuring every GPU phase has work to do.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: expandable scan report panel with per-phase metrics

Clicking 'Last scan' now opens a detailed report panel showing:
- Phase 1 (Discovery): success/failure, items found/updated/skipped
- Phase 2 (Enrichment): tasks processed, success/failed/retried counts
- Phase 3 (Update Check): tasks checked, success/failed/retried counts
- Phase 4 (Consolidation): merge suggestions found
- Per-phase timing (duration in seconds)
- Error details for failed tasks (up to 5 shown)
- Warning indicator on 'Last scan' label if any phase had issues

Data collected in real-time during scan execution and persisted in
localStorage. Report survives page refreshes. Closes on click outside.

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update scripts/twit_scraper.py

  emails & Teams...', '🔄 Updating task...', '✅ 3 result(s) found'
- New updateFrozenStatus() for live badge updates without re-render
- freezeTask() accepts optional statusText parameter

commit.message:
Add SystemVerilog testbenches for RTL primitives library

Create 16 standalone testbench files in testbenches/ directory covering:
- CDC: sync_2ff, async_fifo
- Arbiters: fixed_priority_arbiter, round_robin_arbiter
- Bitops: priority_encoder, popcount, barrel_shifter
- Counters: up_counter, gray_counter
- Memory: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo
- Handshake: skid_buffer
- Pipeline: pipeline_register, delay_line
- Encoding: gray_encoder
- Clock: clock_divider

Each testbench uses `timescale 1ns/1ps, waveform dumps, $error for
failures, PASS/FAIL summary, and tests basic functionality plus corner
cases such as reset, overflow, backpressure, and one-bit Gray transitions.
Testbenches assume DUT modules are compiled separately in the same
compilation unit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: rtl-primitives
repository.description: Open Source RTL primitives library for FPGA and ASIC designers in SystemVerilog

commit.message:
feat: mini-batch interleaving for GPU phase scheduling

Split each chunk's GPU processing into mini-batches of ~100 images.
Instead of processing ALL captions (~3.5h for 500 images) before ANY
objects/faces/embedding, phases are interleaved per mini-batch:

  [caption x100] -> [objects x100] -> [faces+embed x100] -> repeat

Benefits:
- First 100 images fully analyzed in ~40 min (not 3.5h)
- Workers help with caption during fast-module windows
- Model switch overhead: ~18s per boundary (<1% of batch time)

Auto-tunes mini-batch size: min 50, default 100, skip if chunk <= 100.
Adds explicit Ollama unload before deferred perception for safety.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Merge development into release

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add memory RTL primitives: dual_port_ram, simple_fifo, register_file, cam, line_buffer, circular_buffer

- dual_port_ram: true dual-port RAM with independent clocks, registered-read for block RAM inference
- simple_fifo: synchronous FIFO with explicit count register for full/empty detection
- register_file: multi-read-port file with combinational reads and write-to-read forwarding
- content_addressable_memory: fully-associative CAM with priority-encoded hit address
- line_buffer: streaming image line buffer outputting a vertical column of NUM_LINES pixels
- circular_buffer: ring buffer with head/tail pointers, full/empty, and count output

All modules: snake_case signals, UPPER_CASE parameters, always_ff/always_comb,
no initial blocks, no vendor primitives, fully synthesizable.

Make run.sh primary launcher and fix restart flow

- Replace legacy build.py usage with run.sh build+launch loop
- Fix GUI restart handling and robust relaunch on macOS
- Keep rebuild/restart behavior via exit code 43

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: load animation frames alongside v2 layers in browser snapshot

When loadFromBrowserSnapshot receives a v2 layer-based project, it was
returning early after loading layers, skipping the animation.frames
data. This left state.frames as a single empty default frame.

All preview/export tools (get_canvas_ascii, get_canvas_summary,
export_text, etc.) read from state.frames, so they reported the canvas
as empty and exported all-black images.

The browser already sends both layers (v2) and animation.frames (from
the animation store adapter) in every snapshot. Removing the early
return lets both get loaded — layers for v2-aware operations, frames
for preview/export compatibility.

Fixes cameronfoxly/Ascii-Motion#91

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add counters/ directory with 8 synthesizable SystemVerilog primitives

- up_counter: synchronous up counter with enable, load, registered overflow
- down_counter: synchronous down counter with enable, load, registered underflow
- up_down_counter: bidirectional counter with overflow/underflow detection
- saturating_counter: clamps at MAX/MIN with combinational at_max/at_min flags
- gray_counter: binary counter with XOR Gray-code output for CDC use
- event_counter: event accumulator with threshold comparison and sync clear
- watchdog_timer: programmable down-counter watchdog with one-cycle timeout pulse
- programmable_interval_timer: configurable periodic/one-shot tick generator

All modules follow project conventions: snake_case signals, UPPER_CASE
parameters, always_ff/always_comb, active-low synchronous reset (rst_n),
no initial blocks, no vendor-specific primitives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add bitops/ RTL primitive modules

Add 9 fully-synthesizable combinational SystemVerilog modules:
- priority_encoder: lowest-index set bit to binary with valid flag
- leading_zero_counter: MSB leading zero count, width clog2(WIDTH+1)
- trailing_zero_counter: LSB trailing zero count, width clog2(WIDTH+1)
- popcount: adder-tree population count, O(log2 WIDTH) depth
- onehot_encoder: one-hot to binary via OR reduction
- onehot_decoder: binary to one-hot with bounds check
- thermometer_encoder: binary N to N-ones thermometer code
- barrel_shifter: logical/arithmetic left+right, cascaded-mux
- rotate_unit: left/right rotation, cascaded-mux with right->left conversion

All modules verified with pyslang 10.0.0 elaboration: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add handshake RTL primitives (7 SystemVerilog modules)

Introduces a new handshake/ directory with 7 fully synthesizable
SystemVerilog modules implementing common ready/valid handshake patterns:

- ready_valid_stage: single registered pipeline stage with back-pressure
- skid_buffer: 2-entry elastic buffer with registered in_ready
- elastic_buffer: circular FIFO (power-of-2 depth) with combinational head output
- mailbox_fifo: single-entry mailbox supporting simultaneous wr+rd
- credit_flow_controller: sender-side credit counter with registered output
- pipeline_register_slice: fully registered slice (EMPTY/FULL/BOTH FSM)
- backpressure_adapter: absorbs always-valid source into FIFO with overflow flag

All modules follow project conventions: snake_case ports/signals,
UPPER_CASE parameters, always_ff for flip-flops, active-low rst_n,
no initial blocks, no vendor primitives, no combinational ready/valid loops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add CDC primitives library (8 modules)

Add a complete set of synthesizable SystemVerilog CDC primitives:

- sync_2ff: 2-flop metastability synchronizer with DONT_TOUCH/async_reg
- sync_3ff: 3-flop synchronizer for higher-MTBF applications
- pulse_synchronizer: toggle-detect single-cycle pulse CDC
- toggle_synchronizer: multi-bit level/Gray-code bus synchronizer
- req_ack_synchronizer: 4-phase handshake CDC with src_ack feedback
- bundled_data_synchronizer: req/ack-guarded multi-bit data transfer
- gray_pointer_sync: Gray-code pointer 2-FF synchronizer (async FIFO use)
- async_fifo: dual-clock FIFO with Gray-code pointers and full/empty flags

All modules use always_ff, active-low resets (rst_n), snake_case signals,
UPPER_CASE parameters, and include full header documentation with usage
notes and example instantiations.

fix: intelligent duplicate detection with subject normalization and task reactivation

Problem: Scan created duplicate tasks for the same email thread when
subject prefixes differed (e.g. 'Re: RG Netzwerkprüfung' vs
'[EXTERN] RG Netzwerkprüfung'). Done tasks were only skipped, never
reactivated when new activity appeared.

Changes:
- Add stripSubjectPrefixes() to remove Re:, Fw:, AW:, WG:, [EXTERN],
  [EXTERNAL] etc. before comparison
- normalizeForCompare() now preserves German umlauts (äöüß) and strips
  prefixes before lowercasing
- isSimilarTitle() uses dual threshold: Jaccard >0.6 OR subset ratio
  >=0.9 (catches prefix-only differences)
- Safety-net now reactivates done tasks instead of creating duplicates:
  sets status to needs-attention, adds reactivation history entry,
  resets enrichment for fresh content extraction
- Backward-compat dedup uses normalized comparison instead of exact match
- AI scan prompt explicitly instructs to ignore subject prefixes
- Add session lifecycle management and orphan subprocess reaper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Employee Management System with Express/SQLite backend and React/Vite frontend

- Express REST API with better-sqlite3 (v12+ for Node 24 compatibility)
- CRUD endpoints for employees with department filtering
- Rate limiting via express-rate-limit on API routes
- In-memory SQLite for tests via DB_PATH env var
- 11 Jest/supertest tests, all passing
- React/Vite frontend with EmployeeList, EmployeeForm, SearchFilter components

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
